prompt: I'm setting up code quality tooling on a new Nuxt 4 project that uses pnpm. There's currently no Prettier, no ESLint, nothing. The existing code is written in a specific style: 2-space indent, single quotes, no semicolons, trailing commas everywhere, and I'd like a line width around 100. We use TailwindCSS v4 (with the new @theme directive) and have a mix of .ts, .vue, and .css files. Write me a complete biome.jsonc (tailored for this project — not a generic starter) and the package.json scripts I should add. Skip the install command — I'll run `pnpm add -D -E @biomejs/biome` myself. Also briefly call out anything I should know about Biome's Vue support so I'm not surprised.

assertions.text: JS formatter uses single quotes, no semicolons, trailing commas all
assertions.check: javascript.formatter has quoteStyle == 'single' AND semicolons == 'asNeeded' AND trailingCommas == 'all'

assertions.text: Vue files get an override disabling noise-prone rules
assertions.check: overrides contains an entry whose includes matches '**/*.vue' and disables at least one of: style.useConst, style.useImportType, correctness.noUnusedVariables, correctness.noUnusedImports

assertions.text: Nuxt build output is excluded from files.includes
assertions.check: files.includes contains exclusion entries covering .nuxt and .output (either '!' or '!!' patterns)

assertions.text: package.json scripts include format, lint, check, and ci entries
assertions.check: package.json.scripts.json has keys named format, lint, check, and ci that invoke biome subcommands

assertions.text: Response calls out Vue support is experimental
assertions.check: response.md mentions that Biome's Vue support is experimental / partial / limited (key words: experimental, partial, partially, 2.3, v2.3, limited)
